questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
In cold climate, the water in a radiator of a car gets frozen causing damage to the radiator. Ethylene glycol is used as an antifreezing agent. The amount of ethylene glycol that should be added to 5 kg of water to prevent it from freezing at $-7^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ is
(Given: $K_f$ for water $=1.86 \mathrm{~K} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1} \mathrm{~kg}$; Molar mass of ethylene glycol $=62 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ )

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: The mass $(\mathrm{g})$ of NaCl that has to be dissolved to reduce the vapour pressure of 100 g of water by $10 \%\left(\right.$ Molar mass of $\left.\mathrm{NaCl}=58.5 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}\right)$ is

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: A student intended to prepare 1000 mL of a 10 ppm solution of $\mathrm{K}^{+}$from $\mathrm{KCl} . \mathrm{He}$ made appropriate calculations, weighed the salt accordingly and prepared the solution. However, after making the solution, he realized that the salt he used was $\mathrm{KNO}_3$ and not KCl . The concentration of $\mathrm{K}^{+}(\mathrm{ppm})$ in this prepared solution is

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
At room temperature, $\mathrm{NaCl}(\mathrm{s})$ and $\mathrm{KCl}(\mathrm{s})$ were taken in equal masses and dissolved in equal volumes of water in two separate closed containers I and II respectively.
Of the following, correct option/s is/are
options: To compare molarities in (I) and (II), masses of both the solutions need to be known
Molalities cannot be compared without measuring the mass of water added in each case
If (I) and (II) are completely transferred into another container (III), $\left[\mathrm{Cl}^{-}\right]$in (III) will be sum of that in (I) and (II)
Information given is sufficient to compare the vapour pressures in (I) and (II)

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: When 100 g each of the salts $\mathrm{NaCl}, \mathrm{MgSO}_4, \mathrm{Ca}\left(\mathrm{NO}_3\right)_2, \mathrm{~K}_2 \mathrm{CO}_3$, were dissolved separately in 1.0 kg of water, the solution with the highest boiling point will be of

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
A solution of $\mathrm{CuSO}_4 \cdot 5 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ in methanol has $\left[\mathrm{Cu}^{2+}\right]=1.00 \mathrm{mg}$ per 1000 g of methanol. The molarity of $\mathrm{Cu}^{2+}$ in this solution is $\mathrm{Y} \times 10^{-5} \mathrm{~mol} \mathrm{~L}^{-1}$. Y is :
$\left(\right.$ Given- density of methanol $\left.=0.792 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mL}^{-1}\right)$

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
About sea water, which of the following statement/s is/are correct?
I. Frozen sea water melts at a lower temperature than pure ice
II. Boiling point of sea water increases as it evaporates
III. Sea water boils at a lower temperature than fresh water
IV. Density of sea water at STP is same as that of fresh water

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: In reverse osmosis the flow of solvent across semi permeable membrane occurs
options: when hydrostatic pressure is greater than osmotic pressure
when hydrostatic pressure is lower than osmotic pressure
from higher concentrated solution to lower concentrated solution
from lower concentrated solution to higher concentrated solution

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
When a certain amount of a univalent salt AB (molar mass $=54 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ ) was dissolved in $0.1 \mathrm{dm}^3$ of water, the relative lowering of the vapour pressure was found to be $3.55 \%$. The molality of the resulting solution is:
(Assume complete dissociation of the salt under given condition. Density of water $=1 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~cm}^{-3}$ )
